utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five of these items. Each item is fully specified: health_coverage includes type (physical and mental), provider, details, and costs; property_coverage includes what is covered (home/contents with equipment), provider, details, and costs; travel_coverage includes global travel, details, and costs; additional_services lists enhancement services with no cost breakdown but still provides services as required; total_budget includes a complete cost breakdown (monthly/annual totals). All items are therefore covered and fully detailed according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three main insurance components — health_coverage (provider: LifeFirst / HealthFirst), property_coverage (provider: HealthFirst), and travel_coverage (provider: LifeFirst). For each, I must identify the corresponding plan in the ground-truth ratings. The health_coverage combines both LifeFirst and HealthFirst, implying a blend of the 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' and 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage. The property_coverage from HealthFirst maps to 'HomeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection. The travel_coverage from LifeFirst maps to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage. The additional services (telemedicine, gym reimbursement, specialist access, wellness programs, concierge) correspond to listed items in the health_enhancement_services and travel_enhancement_services categories. Specifically: Telemedicine Services (rating 10), Gym Membership Reimbursement (rating 2), Specialist Access (rating 2), Wellness Programs (rating 3), and Concierge Services (rating 1). Therefore, the final mapping uses these corresponding ratings.
